result.lens: Descriptive set theory / Borel chromatic number: irrational-winding phase gadgets (cocycle obstructions over SO(2) as the definable shadow of the L55 imprimitivity route)
result.first_principles:
Ask where R^2 differs from all three controls simultaneously. Q^2 is countable, so every subset is Borel and definability constraints are vacuous (chi_B(Q^2) = chi(Q^2) = 2). R^1 has only proper translation dynamics: unit chains have the clopen transversal [n, n+1), so chi_B(R^1) = 2. The L^infty plane has isometry group R^2 semidirect D_4: its rotation part is FINITE, and chi_B(l_inf^2) = 4 is witnessed by the half-open-square floor-parity coloring, which is Borel. Exactly one feature is unique to Euclidean R^2: SO(2), an infinite COMPACT group preserving unit distance, whose generic elements act by uniquely ergodic irrational rotations. A definable lower bound at 6 must consume SO(2), and the cheapest definable obstruction in mathematics is a cocycle over an irrational rotation: KST's classical chi_B = 3 > 2 = chi for the irrational-rotation graph says bipartite parity is a Z/2 phase that exists abstractly but admits no Borel selection under ergodicity.

Now read the repo's walls through this lens. L55: the only live combinatorial route to chi >= 6 is a wide-interface gadget with an IMPRIMITIVE boundary transfer, i.e. a Z/p phase with p >= 2; width-1 relations are confined to the primitive monoid for k >= 3. At k = 2, J-I is a permutation (imprimitive), and that exception IS the KST theorem: the monoid obstruction and the classical Borel +1 are one fact at different k. The combinatorial payoff of a phase requires closing the chain into a necklace of length not divisible by p, forcing the interface angle theta to be a rational multiple of pi; but unit-distance rigidity puts 2cos(theta) in a number field, and by Niven/Kronecker such angles are almost never rational multiples of pi. The inversion: an UNCLOSED phase chain wound by irrational tau converts any Borel 5-coloring into a Borel f: S^1 -> Z/p with f(x + theta) = f(x) + 1, a measurable eigenfunction with rational eigenvalue for an irrational rotation, which does not exist. The closure wall is the ergodicity hypothesis. The missing object therefore weakens from the dead clamp (L45/L56/L57) to a realizable chain gadget whose 5-color transfer digraph maps onto a directed p-cycle, e.g. an equality-alternator: a 4-vertex predicate invisible to all pair-level scans run so far.

conjectures.name: C1. Phase-Winding Criterion (irrational monodromy transfer theorem)
conjectures.statement: Let P be a finite unit-distance graph with ordered vertex tuples L, R such that R = tau(L) for a Euclidean rotation tau by angle theta with theta/pi irrational, and suppose the bi-infinite chain G = union over n in Z of tau^n(P) (copies vertex-disjoint except the prescribed interface identifications) has nonempty extendable interface-coloring set C at k = 5, with consecutive-compatibility relation T subset of C x C. If T admits a map psi: C -> Z/p with p >= 2 and psi(b) = psi(a) + 1 mod p for EVERY (a,b) in T (equivalently, T is a digraph homomorphic onto the directed p-cycle), then there is no Borel proper 5-coloring of the unit-distance graph on R^2, and no proper 5-coloring all of whose color classes have the Baire property: chi_B(R^2) >= 6 and chi_BP(R^2) >= 6. No-lose clause: if instead C is empty, compactness yields a finite non-5-colorable sub-chain and chi(R^2) >= 6 outright. Proof mechanism: pull the plane coloring back along the Borel bundle map S^1 x Z x V(P) -> R^2, (beta, n, v) -> tau^n R_beta(v); the phase of the copy at angle gamma defines Borel f: S^1 -> Z/p with f(gamma + theta) = f(gamma) + 1, so g = exp(2 pi i f / p) is a measurable eigenfunction of the irrational rotation with eigenvalue exp(2 pi i / p), which is not in the eigenvalue group {exp(i n theta)} for any irrational theta. Contradiction, both for Lebesgue measure and Baire category.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Implement the phase detector (extendable-core pruning of T, then BFS labeling; p = gcd of closed-walk length discrepancies) and run four controls: (1) single unit edge as gadget at k = 2 must give p = 2, reproducing KST chi_B = 3 for the irrational-rotation path bundle; (2) triangle strip (edges v_n v_{n+1}, v_n v_{n+2}) at k = 3 must give p = 3; (3) the 3-rail ladder at k = 3 must give p = 1 (its transfer digraph has both 2-cycles and 3-cycles); (4) a single-port forced-different (J-I) clamp chain at k = 5 must give p = 1, confirming that the L51 abstract clamp carries NO Borel obstruction and that this criterion is genuinely distinct from the clamp route. Any deviation on (1)-(4) falsifies the criterion or its implementation. Independently: exhibiting a Borel 5-coloring of the bundle of any p >= 2 gadget refutes the theorem itself.
conjectures.why_plausible: Each ingredient is standard and verified in isolation: the cocycle non-trivialization over irrational rotations is classical ergodic theory (eigenvalue group of an irrational rotation is {exp(i n theta)}); the pullback of a proper coloring along a graph homomorphism is proper, so extra coincidental unit-distances in the embedded chain only restrict colorings and cannot break the universally quantified phase property; the k = 2 instance is literally the KST theorem. The criterion is the unique common generalization of KST and the L55 imprimitivity diagnostic.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: Three technical leak points: (a) if the extendable core of T has several weakly connected components with inconsistent psi labels, a bi-infinite coloring could slip phase at a one-time SCC descent; the homomorphism-onto-C_p hypothesis is designed to close this but the bi-infinite-walk bookkeeping must be airtight; (b) countably many collision angles where tau^n(P) intersects P off-interface must be shown to affect only a null/meager set of bundle parameters; (c) the criterion requires a UNIFORM chain (same gadget each step); if realizability demands per-step varying flex, equivariance of the bundle fails and the eigenfunction equation is lost.
conjectures.novelty_claim: The HN Borel literature above the trivial 5 <= chi_B <= 7 is essentially empty; nothing ties unit-distance transfer-matrix imprimitivity to chi_B(R^2) >= 6. Nearest prior art: Kechris-Solecki-Todorcevic (irrational rotation graph, the k = 2 germ), Bernshteyn's local-to-Borel transfer (upper-bound/algorithmic direction, not lower bounds via cocycles), and Marks' determinacy method (acyclic Cayley graphs, no Euclidean geometry). The specific bundle construction over SO(2) with a finite UDG fiber gadget appears unstated anywhere.

conjectures.name: C2. Equality-Alternator gadget exists (the weakest sufficient object, strictly below the clamp)
conjectures.statement: There exists a finite unit-distance graph P with at most ~64 vertices, omega(P) <= 3, coordinates in a real number field, with two disjoint NON-adjacent vertex pairs L = (u1, u2) and R = (v1, v2) = tau(L) for an infinite-order rotation tau, realizable as a uniform chain (tau^n(P) disjoint except interface identifications), such that every proper 5-coloring c of P satisfies exactly one of c(u1) = c(u2) and c(v1) = c(v2): the equality predicate ALTERNATES. This is a Z/2 phase on a width-2 interface: blocks B_mono and B_rainbow with T mapping each onto the other. No individual pair is forced-same or forced-different, so it is invisible to the L45/L56/L57 sweeps; the interface has width 2, so it escapes the L55 width-1 monoid confinement. Via C1 it gives chi_B(R^2) >= 6 (or, if the chain is not 5-colorable, chi(R^2) >= 6).
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Decidable per candidate by exactly two SAT calls: forced alternation on (P1, P2) iff 'exists proper 5-coloring with both pairs monochromatic OR both rainbow' is UNSAT, i.e. UNSAT for (eq(P1) AND eq(P2)) and UNSAT for (neq(P1) AND neq(P2)). Run the sweep of the experiment below over lineage subgraphs and spindle-composed assemblies. The conjecture is falsified in stages: (weak) the sweep finds no alternator in any constructible family up to the size bound; (strong) someone proves a width-2 S_5-equivariant analog of the L55 monoid theorem showing mono/rainbow alternation is impossible for omega <= 3 unit-distance graphs at k = 5.
conjectures.why_plausible: The historical ladder used exactly this kind of predicate escalation: k = 3 to 4 used forced pair-equality (the rhombus tip lemma inside the Moser spindle); k = 4 to 5 used de Grey's forced predicate 'some linking triple is monochromatic' (not a forced pair!). The natural k = 5 to 6 step is again a forced PREDICATE one level up, and the equality-alternator is the minimal S_5-invariant predicate on a width-2 interface. L55 shows nothing below width 2 can work, and L57 shows nothing at the pair level exists; the alternator sits exactly in the first unexcluded cell of the hierarchy. The abstract (non-UDG) version looks constructible from 6-critical machinery, paralleling the one-move abstract clamp of L51.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: An unproven equivariant obstruction could kill it: S_5-symmetry plus omega <= 3 might confine width-2 transfer relations to a primitive family the way 2-transitivity confines width-1 (no one has computed the width-2 analog of the L55 monoid; that computation is itself a finite check worth doing). Or the W3 wall could recur one rung down: abstract alternators exist but every one fails unit-distance realizability for the same cocircularity reasons that block the clamp.
conjectures.novelty_claim: I know of no statement of an equality-alternating gadget anywhere in the HN literature; pair-level forcing (this repo's L45-L57) and clique/rainbow forcing (Lemma L lineage) have been studied, but the XOR-of-equality predicate on two non-adjacent pairs has not. Nearest prior art: de Grey 2018's monochromatic-triple forcing predicate, and this repo's L55 route-(ii) framing, neither of which names the object.

conjectures.name: C3. Niven Winding Dichotomy (the wall is the weapon)
conjectures.statement: Let P be any RIGID realizable chain gadget with imprimitive 5-transfer (phase p >= 2) and interface rotation angle theta (rigidity makes theta an isolated algebraic angle: 2cos(theta) is an algebraic number in the gadget's coordinate field). Then exactly one of: (a) theta/pi is rational, theta = 2 pi k / L in lowest terms; if L is not divisible by p, the closed necklace union of tau^n(P), n = 0..L-1, is a FINITE unit-distance graph with no proper 5-coloring (phase monodromy L mod p != 0; coincidence edges only add constraints), hence chi(R^2) >= 6; (b) theta/pi is irrational and C1 gives chi_B(R^2) >= 6 and chi_BP(R^2) >= 6. Therefore the existence of ANY rigid realizable 5-phase gadget implies chi(R^2) >= 6 OR chi_B(R^2) >= 6, except in the single conspiracy case (theta/pi rational with p dividing L). Refinement: branch membership is DECIDABLE in sympy by the Kronecker test (theta/pi rational iff the minimal polynomial of 2cos(theta) divides a real-cyclotomic Chebyshev factor, equivalently all algebraic conjugates of 2cos(theta) lie in [-2, 2] for an algebraic integer; Niven's theorem is the rational special case). Prediction: lineage spindling angles (arccos(5/6), arccos(7/8), the de Grey rotation angles) all land in branch (b), so if phase gadgets live in lineage-type fields the program's terminal state is the L54 dark horse: chi = 5 possibly, chi_B >= 6 definitely.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Two concrete checks. (1) The angle decision procedure: compute minimal polynomials of 2cos(theta) for all distinct interface angles occurring in the 12 lineage graphs and decide rationality of theta/pi exactly; the prediction is irrational for every spindling angle (any rational-pi spindle angle other than the trivial pi/6-family would falsify the refinement and would itself be remarkable). (2) The dichotomy's branch (a) is a finite SAT check whenever instantiated: build the necklace for any rational-angle phase gadget found and verify 5-UNSAT with cadical plus an independent solver. A found phase gadget whose necklace is 5-SAT despite L mod p != 0 falsifies the monodromy argument outright.
conjectures.why_plausible: Both branches reduce to verified components: branch (a) is pigeonhole on the phase potential around a closed walk (extra edges from closure only shrink the coloring set, never break a universally quantified property); branch (b) is C1. The Kronecker/Niven input is classical. The dichotomy also retro-explains the program's experience: closure-type constructions (W3) keep failing precisely because rigid unit-distance angles refuse to be rational multiples of pi, and that refusal is exactly the hypothesis of the ergodic branch. It converts the program's most persistent frustration into a load-bearing assumption.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: The conspiracy case could be generic rather than exceptional: realizability pressure might correlate phase period p with closure length L (both come from the same gadget symmetry), so every realizable rational-angle phase gadget has p dividing L and branch (a) is empty. Flexible (non-rigid) gadgets evade the dichotomy as stated: a flex interval contains rational angles densely, but setting the flex to a rational angle and closing requires re-checking that the flexed gadget keeps the SAME transfer digraph at the closure configuration (colorings are combinatorial, so they do, but vertex collisions at special flex values could identify interface vertices and change C). The 'exactly one of' framing also silently assumes theta is well-defined, i.e. the chain is uniform.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Unstated as far as I know. Polymath16 used irrationality of rotation angles for orbit DENSITY arguments (building bigger graphs), never as the hypothesis powering an ergodic lower bound; Niven's theorem is folklore in the field-arithmetic of spindles. The inversion 'angle irrationality = the reason finite closure fails = exactly the reason the Borel bound succeeds' connects W3 and the definability split in a way I have not seen in the literature or this repo's atlas.

conjectures.name: C4. Lineage Phase-Sterility (sharp negative prediction extending L57 one predicate level up)
conjectures.statement: For every graph in the 12-graph chi-5 de Grey/Polymath lineage (S199 through 874): (i) no two disjoint non-adjacent vertex pairs (P1, P2) have forced equality-XOR or forced equality-correlation in 5-colorings (i.e. for every such quadruple, all four combinations of eq/neq on the two pairs are realized by proper 5-colorings, subject only to local adjacency constraints); and (ii) every width-2 interface transfer digraph cut along a 2-vertex separator template inside a lineage graph has phase p = 1 at k = 5. In words: the lineage is not merely forcing-sterile at the pair level (L57) but PHASE-sterile at the pair-of-pairs level, so the C2 gadget requires a construction principle outside the lineage, exactly as L57 concluded for the clamp.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Directly decided by the experiment below: stage 1 sweeps eq-XOR/eq-correlation candidates (sampling pre-filter, then 2 SAT calls per survivor), stage 2 computes full width-2 transfer digraphs (at most 625 extendability SAT calls each) and runs the phase detector. A SINGLE surviving forced quadruple or p >= 2 interface falsifies C4, and any such falsifier immediately feeds C1/C3 as a candidate chi_B >= 6 engine; so this conjecture is engineered to be valuable in both outcomes.
conjectures.why_plausible: Everything measured about the lineage points to total local freeness: 2.29M pairs all free (L57), the symmetric C6 closure manufactured no forcing (L51), and the lineage graphs were SAT-MINIMIZED for chi >= 5, a pressure that strips redundant rigid structure; a phase needs structured redundancy. Coloring-entropy growth (the L55 lambda_1 observation) suggests lineage transfer digraphs are fat and aperiodic.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: Could fail simply because nobody has looked: pair-of-pairs correlations are a strictly larger search space than pairs and L57's exhaustiveness does not cover them; the sampling pre-filter could also systematically miss low-entropy corners of the coloring space (mitigated by polarity-randomized SAT sampling). Width-3 interfaces and non-separator interface templates are outside the stated scope, so 'phase-sterile' could be true as stated yet misleading.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Novel only as a sharp prediction one level up the predicate hierarchy from this repo's own L57 (the nearest and only prior art); no external literature scans higher-order coloring correlations in the de Grey lineage at all.

experiment.description: One script, experiments/axiomatic/phase_transfer_scan.py, three stages sharing a phase-detector core (extendable-core pruning of the transfer relation, BFS Z-labeling, p = gcd of closed-walk discrepancies; pure networkx, exact). Stage 0 CONTROLS (minutes, decides whether C1's machinery is sound): unit-edge chain at k=2 must give p=2 (KST), triangle strip at k=3 must give p=3, 3-rail ladder at k=3 must give p=1, single-port J-I clamp chain at k=5 must give p=1. Stage 1 LINEAGE EQ-XOR SWEEP (decides C4(i), probes C2): for each of the 12 lineage graphs, draw ~200 polarity-randomized proper 5-colorings via cadical, compute the equality vector over all non-adjacent pairs, keep only quadruples (P1, P2) from Theorem-R cocircular templates whose XOR (or correlation) is constant across all samples, then confirm each survivor with exactly 2 SAT calls (forced-XOR iff both 'eq(P1) AND eq(P2)' and 'neq(P1) AND neq(P2)' are UNSAT). Stage 2 WIDTH-2 TRANSFER DIGRAPHS (decides C4(ii)): for each 2-vertex separator template, compute the <= 25x25 transfer digraph with <= 625 extendability SAT calls, run the phase detector, and for any p >= 2 hit compute the exact interface angle and its Kronecker rational-pi test (sympy minpoly of 2cos theta) to assign the C3 branch. Decision rule: any p >= 2 hit with irrational angle escalates to VERIFIER as a chi_B >= 6 candidate engine; a clean sweep confirms C4 and redirects C2 search to spindle-composed families outside the lineage. Verification targets for VERIFIER: (a) formalize C1's cocycle argument (the eigenvalue-group step is 10 lines of ergodic theory); (b) reproduce any UNSAT with a second solver. Adversarial targets for ADVERSARY: attack C1's multi-component and collision-angle leak points; attempt a width-2 equivariant monoid theorem killing C2.
experiment.repo_feasibility: Everything reuses existing plumbing: lineage graph loaders and SAT harness from shotC_forced_same.py / shotD_exhaustive_forcing.py, cadical via python-sat, sympy for exact angles and minimal polynomials, networkx for the digraph work. No C compiler, no nauty, no new dependencies. The 625-call transfer computation is the same extendability pattern already used in wildcard_b_rg_transfer.py, one width up.
experiment.est_cost: ~1 day of implementation. Stage 0: minutes. Stage 1: overnight (sampling kills almost all candidates; expect under ~10^3 confirming SAT calls per graph at ~1-10 s each). Stage 2: hours (<= 625 SAT calls per template, ~50-100 curated templates). Total: one coding day plus one overnight compute run.

detector_self_check.Q2: Q^2 is a countable metric space, so every subset is Borel and chi_B(Q^2) = chi(Q^2) = 2; the criterion cannot over-prove there because its engine is a nonatomic uniquely ergodic rotation on an uncountable bundle base, and on a countable space phases are Borel-selectable pointwise by enumeration (no choice needed). The k=2 parity phase exists along Q^2 chains too (Q^2 even contains infinite-order rational rotations like arctan(3/4)), but with countable orbits and no nonatomic invariant measure the eigenfunction contradiction never gets off the ground. The Euclidean content enters exactly where Q^2 fails: a continuum of parallel fibers requiring a simultaneous measurable phase selection.
detector_self_check.Linf: Two independent firewalls. First, structural inapplicability: the criterion consumes an infinite-order unit-distance-preserving rotation, and Isom(l_inf^2) has rotation part D_4 (order 8), so no irrational winding exists in the L^infty plane at all; chains there move by translations, which act properly with a Borel transversal, so phases are selectable and the known Borel 4-coloring (half-open unit squares colored by floor parity, proper because an L^infty distance of exactly 1 forces a floor-parity flip in some coordinate) stands untouched: chi_B(l_inf^2) = 4, no over-proof of >= 5. Second, realizability of the wide cocircular interfaces in C2/C3 runs through Theorem R's two-unit-circles-meet-in-at-most-2-points rigidity, which is false in L^infty where unit spheres intersect in segments. This proposal was designed specifically to replace the norm-blind Steinhaus blade that sank the L54 borel world; the blade here is a cocycle over SO(2), which simply does not exist in L^infty.
detector_self_check.R1: The argument is rotation-CONSUMING, not rotation-blind: its entire engine is the eigenvalue group of an irrational element of SO(2). Isom(R) contains no infinite compact subgroup; unit-distance components of R^1 are proper Z-translation orbits with the clopen transversal [n, n+1), so every phase (including the k=2 parity) is Borel-selectable and chi_B(R^1) = 2 is respected. Instantiating the criterion's hypothesis on the line is impossible because there is nothing to wind around; the bundle base S^1 with dense irrational orbits is a strictly 2-dimensional Euclidean phenomenon.
